description:
On December 20, 2024 at 8:28 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (“Waymo AV”) operating 
in Los Angeles, CA was in a collision involving two SUVs, a passenger car, and a pick up truck 
on Bundy Dr at West Olympic Boulevard. 
The Waymo AV was stopped in autonomous mode facing south at a red light as the lead vehicle 
in the second from the right lane when an SUV traveling northbound in an oncoming traffic lane 
ran the red light. As the oncoming SUV entered the intersection, it made contact with a 
passenger car traveling east on West Olympic Boulevard. The oncoming SUV continued 
through the intersection and swerved to narrowly miss a pedestrian before making contact with 
the front of the Waymo AV and the front driver side of a stationary pickup truck to the right of the 
Waymo AV. The impact from the oncoming SUV caused the Waymo AV to contact another SUV 
that was also stationary to the left of the Waymo AV at the red traffic light. The Waymo AV test 
driver transitioned to manual mode immediately before the oncoming SUV made contact. At the 
time of the impact, the Waymo AV’s Level 4 ADS was not engaged and a test driver was 
operating the Waymo AV in manual mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage to the front and 
rear of the vehicle, necessitating a tow from the scene. The oncoming SUV sustained damage 
to the front and driver side. The stationary SUV that was contacted sustained damage to the 
passenger side. The Waymo test driver was transported to a local hospital for medical 
treatment.
